name: Alcala la Real - return via Frailes
name_original: Alcalá la Real – vuelta por Frailes
region_intro: Road cycling around Alcalá La Real offers a diverse environment characterized by challenging terrain and scenic landscapes. Situated between the Guadalquivir Valley and the plains of Granada, the region features numerous mountain ranges, extensive olive groves, and river valleys. Cyclists encounter varied elevations, from demanding climbs to more moderate paths, providing a rich experience for different fitness levels. The area's physical features include prominent hills like the one hosting Fortaleza de la Mota, contributing to its varied topography. Best road cycling routes in Alcalá La Real The most popular road cycling route is Alcalá la Real – return via Castillo de Locubín and Alcaudete, a 42.3 miles (68.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 33 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alcala la Real - return via Frailes, a difficult 26.4 miles (42.5 km) path. This route includes substantial climbs and descends through the region's hilly terrain. Local road cyclists also love the View of Alcalá la Real – Puerto del Castillo loop from Alcalá la Real, a 34.5 miles (55.5 km) trail leading through the surrounding hills and offering panoramic views, often completed in about 3 hours 19 minutes. Road cycling around Alcalá La Real is defined by its surrounding mountain ranges, endless olive groves, and historic viewpoints like the Fortaleza de la Mota. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Alcalá La Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 7 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcalá La Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Alcalá La Real?
A: Alcalá La Real offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 50 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to challenging climbs through the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in Alcalá La Real?
A: The terrain around Alcalá La Real is characterized by a compelling blend of challenging climbs and scenic landscapes. Situated between the Guadalquivir Valley and the plains of Granada, you'll encounter varied elevations, including prominent hills like the one hosting Fortaleza de la Mota, extensive olive groves, and several mountain ranges such as Hoya de Charilla and Sierra de la Martina.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Alcalá La Real?
A: Yes, there are easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38284619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascante Tower – Alcalá la Real loop from Fuente del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 27.2 km with a moderate elevation gain, making it a good choice for those new to road cycling in the area.

Q: What are some challenging long-distance road cycling routes in Alcalá La Real?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996228933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá la Real – return via Castillo de Locubín and Alcaudete&lt;/a&gt; (68.1 km) and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1285997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alcalá la Real – Puerto del Castillo loop from Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt; (55.5 km) offer significant elevation gains and varied landscapes. These routes demand a good level of fitness.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Alcalá La Real?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14622917" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alcalá la Real – Alcalá la Real loop from Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 47.7 km ride that takes you through the surrounding areas.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Alcalá La Real often pass by stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You can encounter the impressive sharp-cut rocks of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3230098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Tajos&lt;/a&gt;, the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3229479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Marcos Rock Sculpture Forest&lt;/a&gt;, and the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zurreón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The iconic Fortaleza de la Mota also dominates the skyline, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible or visible from road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region features several beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zurreón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3155607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Caballo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; are notable natural attractions in the vicinity, and some routes may offer views or access points to these natural wonders.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Alcalá La Real?
A: The best times for road cycling in Alcalá La Real are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. The town's elevated position also offers some relief from summer heat compared to lower-lying areas, making it a viable option during warmer months, though early mornings or late afternoons are recommended.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Alcalá La Real?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse environment, the challenging yet rewarding terrain, and the scenic landscapes that include endless olive groves and historic viewpoints like the Fortaleza de la Mota.

Q: Are there options for bike rental or repair in Alcalá La Real?
A: Yes, the local cycling community in Alcalá La Real is supported by services such as Ciclos Ibáñez, which can assist with bike rental or repair needs, ensuring you have everything required for your ride.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Alcalá La Real?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, there are options suitable for families looking for a more relaxed ride. The region offers some moderate paths, and it's advisable to check the difficulty grade and elevation profiles of specific routes to find those best suited for family outings.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Alcalá La Real?
A: Parking is generally available within Alcalá La Real, especially near the town center or starting points of popular routes. It's recommended to check local parking regulations and availability, particularly during peak seasons, to ensure a smooth start to your ride.
tips: Located on top of a hill overlooking the city at a height of 1,029 meters. The castle is a remnant of a medieval fortress dating from the 13th-14th centuries. La Fortaleza de la Mota marked the border between the kingdoms of Granada and Castilla.

Cylindrical watchtower 13 m high dating from the 15th century, located southeast of Alcalá la Real, on Cascante hill, a strategic place on the Camino de los Frailes near the border with Granada. Twelve of the fifteen watchtowers that protect the castle remain.

waypoints.name: View of the Fortaleza de la Mota
